string 2: Dad!
Inori, is that you?!
Why would you make something like this?!
Am I dreaming...? I can't believe my eyes...

There's so much I want to tell you, but... I need you to
explain first!
Dad... what in the world are you trying to do?
Inori... come here.
You always hated your public safety work...
...so this doesn't make sense! Tell me why!
I... thought you...
Ngh...
...Apprehend the intruders.
What?! Dad!
...Suspend the experiment. Time for a change of plans.

Our schedule can't handle delays like that. The operation to
eliminate all the Digimon around Shinjuku is tomorrow, yes?

Quite the opposite. We're going to push forward to the execution
phase. There doesn't seem to be time to spare anymore.

Some odd readings were confirmed above the Tokyo
Metropolitan Government Building just now.

They're identical to those from eight years ago... when the
Digimon hordes appeared.
If that thing opens, then we'll be swarmed by Digimon.
Oh, no... Do they mean a gate?

That reminds me... The Titans said they were launching an 
all-out attack!
If things keep going this way...
We'll have a true war on our hands.{next}
The Shinjuku Inferno will take place.{next}
It'll be the end of the world.{next}

Head to the skies above the government building and engage
the enemy.

Don't let the tragedy of eight years ago happen again. Make
sure they can't take anything more that's dear to us.
That's what we've given everything to achieve.
Roger! Over and out!
Captain Kuremi, this is for you...!

I realized something when you vanished. I realized that life
has done nothing but take, take, take...
And you know who's behind all of it? Digimon.

When protecting what matters... sometimes you have to
get your hands dirty.
Dad!

...Attack! Eliminate any potential hostiles that appear on
the roof!
You heard him! Go on ahead!

I'll be fine! I'll get through to my dad and be right behind
you! I promise!
Inori!
Wait!
There...!
Is that Digimon headed for the same place?
We'd better take a little detour so they don't spot us!
But if we do, we might not make it in time...!
Sounds like we should split up.{next}

Huh? You mean I take the detour, and you go the same
way as that Digimon?
...Yeah, that just might be our best option.

That way, at least one of us might make it, instead of
neither of us if we go together!
...Okay, but we have to meet up again later. Promise me!

While evading public safety forces, you become separated from
Aegiomon. Somehow, you manage to escape to the surface...

Be careful. Your current coordinates are near ground level 
and inside the Wall. It might be the Digimon District.

Don't you recognize that Digimon just now?
It's like we're reliving the events of that day.

That public safety weapon is also what caused the Shinjuku
Inferno. It seems this timeline is also on the path to ruin.

We're out of time. We have no choice but to go with what
we feel to be the best option at our disposal.
...even if sacrifices have to be made!

That means erasing the most plausible source of the anomaly.
Go at once and eliminate Aegiomon, along with Inori Misono.
Please, just give us a little more time!{next}
There's no way I could do that!{next}
Those two... are my family now.{next}

Do you have any idea what you're saying? Have you
forgotten the very fate of the world is at stake?
...Oh, boy.

Well, if my ground operative refuses to take action,
there's not much I can do on my end.
I predicted you wouldn't comply with this order. After all...
...I've known you for quite some time now.
Fine, then... We're in this through thick and thin, aren't we.

Of course, we won't go unpunished for disobeying orders
from above... Still, I'm with you to the end.

I'll buy us as much time as I can. Just remember there's a
limit to how much I can do.
For now... do whatever it takes to prevent the Shinjuku Inferno!
We can worry about what comes after when the time comes.

There ya are! I have an urgent message! Just promise ya'll
stay calm while ya listen...

We got a report that, after venturing into the Digital
World... Merukimon died after a fierce battle.

With death by poison looming and our forces in Central Town
dwindling, he sacrificed himself so his allies could escape.

This allowed us to seal the Rebellion Village gate, but I
doubt the enemy will give up on invading the human world.

They'll likely seek another gate, but we'll fight to the bitter
end with everyone who's left. This is about revenge!

Inori Misono's father just said a gate is opening above the
Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building...

That may be the Titans' point of entry... The forces that
defeated Merukimon are on the warpath!

We're also detecting indicators of a space-time disturbance.
At this rate... it's going to trigger the Inferno!
I understand why you'd feel shaken, but you need to stay calm.

What we need to look out for above all else... is a
space-time disturbance—

—the anomaly that erased your adoptive father, "Dr. Yuki",
from existence.

The Inferno triggers a rash of such disturbances across
the planet, and they lead directly to the end of the world.

Space-time disturbances are a constant threat here in my
timeline. Get caught in one and you'll never make it home.
Hello down there!

...Are you all right?! You'll be squashed flat if you get
stepped on!
We'll give you a lift!
Hang on tight! Here we go!
This is bad...! I'm already detecting a space-time disturbance!
A-Aaaaagh...!
Inoriiiii!
What's going on?!
Ngh!
Ngrrrrrrrgh...!
This place...!
No!
I'm being sucked in...!
Inori!
Hnnnh...!
Eeeeeeek!
Inoriiiiiii!
Ngh...
U-Unnnnngh...!
Aaaaaaaaagh!
Hrrrrrnh...!
First time here alone, isn't it?

As for your two friends... It seems they fell into a crack
in time and space.

You folks call them "space-time disturbances."
I don't know anyone who's ever escaped from one.

Do you know what the cause of today's tragedy is?
There's a point in time where you made the wrong choice.

The Akashic Vision will play even if there's only one
audience member. Go see, with your own two eyes.
Merukimon, a warrior with unparalleled swiftness and strength.

Merukimon's valor drew Digimon to him, and they eventually
formed a small village over time.

Before long, this village evolved into a large city with vibrant,
bustling streets.

But even this lively and peaceful city was not safe from the
conflict with the Titans.

Merukimon rallied the village Digimon and fought valiantly, but
was struck by a poisoned arrow while protecting a resident.
The wounded Merukimon put up a desperate resistance...

...but his strength finally failed in the face of the advancing
enemy horde, and the Titans conquered the city.

Its citizens, burned out of house and home, were scattered
aimlessly to the winds.

Merukimon and his allies took flight, eventually finding their
way to the human world.

There they established a new base, where they awaited the
opportunity to retake their former home.

However, the toxins from the poisoned arrow continued to eat away 
at Merukimon's body, weakening him before the upcoming battle.

Mustering his last bit of strength, Merukimon and his allies
attempted to storm their lost home...
...only to be defeated at the hands of the enemy's formidable forces.

As the poison sapped away his strength, Merukimon, resigned
to his death, sacrificed himself so his allies could flee.

There he fell, the dream of reclaiming the city lost along with
their leader's life.
